title: Systems and methods for on-site recovery of autonomous vehicles
abstract: Systems and methods for recovering an autonomous vehicle in a fleet of vehicles are provided. In one example embodiment, a computer-implemented method includes detecting an existence of an adverse condition associated with an autonomous vehicle in the fleet. The method includes determining in response to detecting the adverse condition, a recovery plan for the first autonomous vehicle based at least in part on one or more attributes associated with the adverse condition, the recovery plan including one or more actions to recover the first autonomous vehicle at a remote location. The method includes initiating the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle at the remote location.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method for recovering an autonomous vehicle in a fleet of vehicles, the method comprising: detecting, by a computing system that includes one or more computing devices, an existence of an adverse condition associated with a first autonomous vehicle in the fleet; determining, by the computing system, in response to detecting the adverse condition, a recovery plan for the first autonomous vehicle based at least in part on one or more attributes associated with the adverse condition, the recovery plan including one or more actions to recover the first autonomous vehicle at a remote location; and initiating, by the computing system, the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle at the remote location. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein initiating, by the computing system, the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle comprises: selecting, by the computing system, a recovery team to perform on-site recovery of the first autonomous vehicle based at least in part on the one or more attributes associated with the adverse condition; and sending, by the computing system, the recovery team to the remote location to recover the first autonomous vehicle. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, wherein sending, by the computing system, the recovery team to the remote location to recover the first autonomous vehicle comprises: sending, by the computing system, a second autonomous vehicle in the fleet to transport the recovery team to the remote location. 4. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the adverse condition is associated with at least one of a component failure in the first autonomous vehicle, an environment proximate to the first autonomous vehicle, or a user associated with the first autonomous vehicle. 5. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein detecting the existence of the adverse condition comprises: 

 receiving, by the computing system, data indicative of the adverse condition from the first autonomous vehicle or a user device corresponding to a user associated with the first autonomous vehicle. 6. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein determining, by the computing system, in response to detecting the adverse condition, the recovery plan for the first autonomous vehicle comprises: obtaining, by the computing system, data associated with one or more recovery teams, the data associated with the one or more recovery teams including a location of the one or more recovery teams; and determining, by the computing system, the recovery plan based at least in part on the remote location and the location of one or more recovery teams. 7. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more attributes further include at least one of a characteristic of at least a portion of a surrounding environment of the first autonomous vehicle, a vehicle service associated with the first autonomous vehicle, or a user associated with the first autonomous vehicle. 8. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein initiating, by the computing system, the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle at the remote location comprises: sending, by the computing system, one or more control signals to the first autonomous vehicle, the control signals comprising one or more vehicle maneuvers to be executed by the first autonomous vehicle. 9. The computer-implemented method of any of the preceding claims, wherein initiating, by the computing system, the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle comprises: providing, by the computing system, data indicative of the adverse condition and the recovery plan for display via one or more display devices onboard the first autonomous vehicle. 10. A computing system for recovering an autonomous vehicle, the system comprising: 

 one or more processors; and one or more tangible, non-transitory, computer readable media that collectively store instructions that when executed by the one or more processors cause the computing system to perform operations, the operations comprising: detecting an existence of an adverse condition associated with a first autonomous vehicle in a fleet of vehicles; determining, in response to detecting the adverse condition, a recovery plan for the first autonomous vehicle based at least in part on one or more attributes associated with the adverse condition, the recovery plan including one or more actions to recover the first autonomous vehicle at a remote location; and initiating the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle at the remote location. 11. The computing system of claim 10, wherein initiating the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle comprises: selecting a recovery team to perform on-site recovery of the first autonomous vehicle based at least in part on the one or more attributes associated with the adverse condition; and sending the recovery team to the remote location to recover the first autonomous vehicle. 12. The computing system of claim 11, wherein sending the recovery team to the remote location to recover the first autonomous vehicle comprises: sending a second autonomous vehicle in the fleet to transport a human operator to the remote location. 13. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein the adverse condition is associated with at least one of a component failure in the first autonomous vehicle, an environment proximate to the first autonomous vehicle, or a user associated with the first autonomous vehicle. 14. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein detecting the existence of the adverse condition comprises: receiving data indicative of the adverse condition from the first autonomous vehicle or a user device corresponding to a user associated with the first autonomous vehicle. 15. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein determining the recovery plan for the first autonomous vehicle comprises: obtaining data associated with one or more recovery teams, the data associated with the one or more recovery teams including a location of the one or more recovery teams; and determining the recovery plan based at least in part on the remote location and the location of one or more recovery teams. 16. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more attributes include at least one of a characteristic of at least a portion of a surrounding environment of the first autonomous vehicle, a vehicle service associated with the first autonomous vehicle, or a user associated with the first autonomous vehicle. 17. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein initiating the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle at the remote location comprises: sending one or more control signals to the first autonomous vehicle, the control signals comprising one or more vehicle maneuvers to be executed by the first autonomous vehicle. 18. The computing system of any of the preceding claims, wherein initiating the recovery plan to recover the first autonomous vehicle comprises: providing data indicative of the adverse condition and the recovery plan for display via one or more display devices onboard the first autonomous vehicle. 19. An autonomous vehicle comprising: one or more processors; and one or more tangible, non-transitory, computer readable media that collectively store instructions that when executed by the one or more processors cause the one or more processors to perform operations, the operations comprising: monitoring one or more parameters associated with the autonomous vehicle; detecting an adverse condition associated with the autonomous vehicle based at least in part on the one or more parameters; stopping a motion of the autonomous vehicle in response to detecting the adverse condition; 

 sending one or more communications to a remote computing system, the one or more communications including a request for assistance with the adverse condition; receiving, from the remote computing system, data indicative of a recovery plan that includes a recovery team being sent to recover the autonomous vehicle at a location where the autonomous vehicle is stopped; and displaying data indicative of the adverse condition and the recovery plan via one or more display devices onboard the autonomous vehicle. 20. The autonomous vehicle of claim 19, further comprising: receiving, from the remote computing system, one or more control signals comprising one or more vehicle maneuvers to be executed by the autonomous vehicle; executing the one or more vehicle maneuvers; determining an existence of the adverse condition after executing the one or more vehicle maneuvers; and sending data indicative of the determination to the remote computing system.
